# AWS Network Firewall — stateful rules (Suricata format) for the centralized
# inspection VPC. Referenced from network-config.yaml:
#   networkFirewall.rules[].ruleGroup.rulesSource.rulesFile = firewall-rules/rules.txt
#
# Platform note: unlike the OpenTofu/CDK/tfvars formats — which attach the
# AWS-managed strict-order stateful rule groups by ARN
# (AWSManagedRulesBotNetCommandAndControlDomainsStrictOrder, AWSManagedRulesMalwareDomainsStrictOrder, AWSManagedRulesAbusedLegitMalwareDomainsStrictOrder) —
# the Landing Zone Accelerator authors stateful rules in this file. This baseline
# gives egress visibility (alert on established flows) plus a sample domain block
# and an explicit allow; operators MUST extend it with organization egress policy
# (or layer in the AWS managed domain lists via a managed rule group reference in
# their LZA version) to reach blocking parity with the other formats.

# Visibility: alert on every established outbound flow (feeds firewall logs).
alert ip any any -> any any (msg:"egress-established-flow"; flow:established,to_server; sid:1000001; rev:1;)

# Sample malicious-domain block (replace/extend with your threat feed).
drop tls any any -> any any (msg:"blocked-egress-domain"; tls.sni; content:"example-malware-domain.invalid"; nocase; endswith; sid:1000002; rev:1;)

# Allow remaining egress (tighten per least-privilege as workloads are defined).
pass ip any any -> any any (msg:"allow-egress"; sid:1000003; rev:1;)
